# ANALYSIS: LIVE_PRICE_FIRST_BAR_TRIGGER Stage-2 implications

The trigger exhibits extreme rarity in historical proxies but shows higher frequency in the relevant premarket sample. The PDL/PDH proxy (0.3% over 343 days) is misleading as the 5/15 case used PML. Actual PML/PMH scan on 77 days with premarket data shows 19 events (24.7% hit rate), though 245/342 days lack premarket bars in the CSV (yfinance limitation). True 16-month frequency is unknown, but upper-bound estimates suggest 1-2 BEAR and 2-3 BULL events per quarter (4-20 annual events). Crucially, zero J anchor days are affected (OP-16 floor intact), meaning the trigger only impacts non-J days.

If true frequency is 1-3 events/quarter (4-12 annual), maximum yearly impact depends on per-event P&L. The motivating case (5/15) yielded +$0.49/share but lacks contract sizing. Assuming similar magnitude to J winners ($342-$730 for multi-contract trades) and conservative 1-contract scaling, annual upside could reach $4,104-$8,760. However, without Stage-1 backtesting on non-J days, this remains speculative. Downside risk is limited by the trigger's structure (reversal bar requirement) but unknown without data.

The candidate should remain NEEDS-MORE-DATA, not WATCH-ONLY. OP-21 requires 3+ live fires for live-bar trigger classes—a feasible target given quarterly estimates (1-5 events/quarter). Reclassifying to WATCH-ONLY would forfeit OP-16 eligibility prematurely; the trigger's zero impact on J days means it cannot hurt edge_capture, and potential additive edge justifies pursuit. Current smoke test passes (7/7), but live-fires and real-fills validation are gating factors.

Watch-first accumulation triggers on observing the premarket level breach with reversal: BEAR_PML_V_REVERSAL (price breaks below PML then closes above it within the same bar) or BULL_PMH_V_REVERSAL (breaks above PMH then closes below). Accumulation begins after the first live fire with real-fills validation, targeting 3 observed events to satisfy OP-21. Concentration risk is low a priori (no J-day exposure), but post-accumulation analysis must verify non-J day P&L distribution to avoid false positives from sparse sampling. Until then, treat as high-variance, low-frequency additive signal requiring rigorous out-of-sample testing. ---
